input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI targeting Android + Windows on Windows 11.

---

## Step 1 — Install the .NET SDK with MAUI workload

```powershell
# Install latest .NET SDK (via winget)
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9

# Verify
dotnet --version

# Install the MAUI workload
dotnet workload install maui

# Confirm installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## Step 2 — Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target)

MAUI Windows apps need the Windows App SDK and WinUI tooling. The simplest path:

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community

# Then add required components via the VS Installer CLI:
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" `
  modify --installPath "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.Maui.All `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetMobile `
  --quiet --norestart
```

## Step 3 — Install Java JDK (Android build dependency)

```powershell
# Discover available OpenJDK versions
winget search Microsoft.OpenJDK

# Install JDK 17 (required by current Android tooling)
winget install Microsoft.OpenJDK.17

# Verify
java -version

# Set JAVA_HOME if not auto-set
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable(
  "JAVA_HOME",
  (Get-ChildItem "C:\Program Files\Microsoft\jdk-*" | Sort-Object Name -Descending | Select-Object -First 1).FullName,
  "User"
)
```

## Step 4 — Install Android SDK & emulator

```powershell
# The MAUI workload installs android SDK pieces, but verify/complete setup:
# Find where dotnet put the Android SDK
$androidSdk = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk"
if (!(Test-Path $androidSdk)) {
    $androidSdk = "$env:USERPROFILE\.android\sdk"  # alternate location
}

# Ensure cmdline-tools are available
$sdkmanager = Get-ChildItem -Recurse "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools" -Filter "sdkmanager.bat" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue |
              Select-Object -First 1

# If missing, bootstrap cmdline-tools manually:
if (!$sdkmanager) {
    $toolsUrl = "https://dl.google.com/android/repository/commandlinetools-win-11076708_latest.zip"
    Invoke-WebRequest $toolsUrl -OutFile "$env:TEMP\cmdline-tools.zip"
    Expand-Archive "$env:TEMP\cmdline-tools.zip" -DestinationPath "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools"
    Rename-Item "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\cmdline-tools" "latest"
    $sdkmanager = "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat"
}
```

```powershell
# Discover available platform & build-tools versions dynamically
& $sdkmanager --list | Select-String "platforms;android-" | Select-Object -Last 10
& $sdkmanager --list | Select-String "build-tools;" | Select-Object -Last 10

# Install required components (adjust versions from output above)
& $sdkmanager --install `
  "platforms;android-35" `
  "build-tools;35.0.0" `
  "platform-tools" `
  "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" `
  "emulator"

# Accept all licenses
& $sdkmanager --licenses   # answer 'y' to all
```

```powershell
# Set ANDROID_HOME
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME", $androidSdk, "User")
```

## Step 5 — Create an Android emulator

```powershell
$avdmanager = $sdkmanager -replace 'sdkmanager','avdmanager'

# List available device definitions
& $avdmanager list device | Select-String "id:" | Select-Object -First 15

# Create an AVD
& $avdmanager create avd `
  --name "Pixel_MAUI" `
  --package "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" `
  --device "pixel_7"

# Launch (optional, to verify)
& "$androidSdk\emulator\emulator.exe" -avd Pixel_MAUI -no-snapshot-load
```

## Step 6 — Enable Windows Developer Mode (Windows target)

```powershell
# Required for sideloading WinUI/MAUI apps
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
  /t REG_DWORD /f /v AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense /d 1
```

## Step 7 — Verify everything works

```powershell
# Create a new MAUI project
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Build for Windows
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Android emulator
dotnet build -f net9.0-android -t:Run

# Run on Windows
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0 -t:Run
```

## Step 8 — Check for version mismatches

```powershell
# Useful diagnostics if builds fail
dotnet --info                          # SDK, runtimes, workloads
dotnet workload list                   # installed workloads
& $sdkmanager --list_installed         # Android SDK components
java -version                          # JDK version
```

---

**Key version requirements summary:**
| Component | Minimum | How to discover |
|---|---|---|
| .NET SDK | 9.0+ | `dotnet --version` |
| JDK | 17 | `java -version` |
| Android API | 21+ (target 35) | `sdkmanager --list` |
| Windows TFM | 10.0.19041.0+ | Check `.csproj` TargetFrameworks |

All commands are idempotent — safe to re-run if something fails partway through.
